title: Reflecting a function in the y-axis
problem: The graph of $y = x^3 - 2$ is reflected in the $y$-axis. Write the equation of the new graph.

steps.instruction: Recall the rule for reflection in the $y$-axis: replace every $x$ in the equation with $-x$.
Simplify $(-x)^3$ using the rule $(-x)^n = -x^n$ for odd $n$.

title: Identifying a translation from the equation $y - b = f(x - a)$
problem: The function $f(x) = x^2$ is transformed to give $y + 5 = f(x - 3)$. Write the equation of the new graph, state the coordinates of the vertex, and state the domain and range.

steps.instruction: Rearrange into the standard form $y = f(x - a) + b$ by isolating $y$.
Read off the translations: $a = 3$ (right by 3) and $b = -5$ (down by 5).
steps.working: $$\text{The vertex of } y = x^2 \text{ is } (0, 0). \text{ It moves to } (0 + 3,\; 0 - 5) = (3, -5).$$

steps.instruction: State the domain. A parabola is defined for all real $x$.
State the range. The parabola opens upward with vertex $y$-value $-5$, so $y$ cannot go below $-5$.

title: Applying a sequence of two transformations
problem: Starting from $y = \sqrt{x}$, apply (i) a reflection in the $x$-axis, then (ii) a translation of 4 units to the right. Write the equation of the resulting graph and state its domain and range.

steps.instruction: Apply the reflection in the $x$-axis first. Replace $y$ with $-y$, which is equivalent to multiplying the right-hand side by $-1$.
Apply the translation of 4 units to the right to the result from Step 1. Replace $x$ with $(x - 4)$.
State the domain. The expression under the square root must be non-negative.
State the range. Since $\sqrt{x - 4} \geq 0$, multiplying by $-1$ gives $y \leq 0$.
